title: Publish acknowledgement outcome ledger surface

outcomes.proof_rule: The publish attempt becomes committed when replay shows the event after the BF-05 timeout window and the attempt ledger can map that replayed identity back to the client write.
outcomes.operator_action: Treat the write as durable and use stable event identity to reconcile any client retry.

outcomes.proof_rule: The publish attempt is rejected when the ledger records rejection and replay never shows the event crossing the durable boundary.
outcomes.operator_action: Retry or surface failure because the event never committed.

outcomes.proof_rule: The publish attempt remains unknown_commit when the client timed out inside the fault window and current replay plus audit evidence has not yet resolved committed versus rejected.
outcomes.operator_action: Keep the ambiguity explicit until replay and audit evidence can resolve the final durable outcome.

limitations: This is a checked-in deterministic stub proof, not a live broker-backed runtime report.
Future live adapters still need provider-native publish ledgers and replay captures that satisfy the same BF-05 classification rules.
unknown_commit remains a first-class reviewer verdict until live evidence can close the ambiguity window deterministically.
